choose: Need exactly ONE specific mod -> essence (or the bench, if the right affix slot is open).
Need a themed COMBINATION of mods (phys weapon, ES chest, minion helm) -> fossils: they raise whole mod classes and can block polluting ones.
Need to FIX HALF an item (good suffixes, bad prefixes) -> Harvest 'reforge keeping suffixes/prefixes' (maps only).
About to GAMBLE on a good magic item (regal, +1/+1 wand) -> beastcraft imprint first, restore on a brick.
Need FREE gear while leveling -> Rog (Expedition): take his offers on the weakest slots.
Need IMPLICITS -> eldritch embers/ichors, endgame maps only.
Everything else -> plain currency plus the bench, in the standard order of operations.

stages.default: vendor recipes for weapons, transmute/alt/aug magics, essences for one-mod gear, bench to fill, Rog offers when Expedition appears; fossils are reachable from Act 4 (Niko, level 14+) if you delve on the way
stages.skip: harvest, eldritch, veiled orbs — not available yet; don't hoard currency for them

stages.default: essence + bench remains the workhorse; add Harvest reforges (Sacred Grove portals in maps) — 'reforge [tag] keeping suffixes' is the best upgrade-in-place; unveil Jun items for better bench options; fossil themes for weapon/ES slots
stages.skip: multi-step metacrafts — gear turns over too fast to deserve them

stages.default: fossil + resonator combos, veiled mods, eldritch implicits on settled gear, imprint-protected regal gambles; recombinators if 3.29 has them
stages.skip: nothing categorically — but keep the stop-loss guideline in force

methods.name: Currency + bench
methods.use_when: The default fabric of every craft; see order of operations.
methods.avoid_when: Chaos-spamming for a specific mod — an essence does that job with a guarantee.

methods.name: Essences
methods.what: Full reroll with one guaranteed mod; upgrades normal or rerolls rare
methods.where: drops everywhere from Act 1; low tiers have item-level caps (digest shows them)
methods.use_when: One non-negotiable mod: life, a resist, flat damage, minion life. The league-start default for every gear slot.
methods.avoid_when: Two+ specific mods wanted together — pair with bench or move to fossils.

methods.name: Fossils + resonators (Delve)
methods.what: Biased reroll: raises themed mod classes, can block others; resonators combine fossils
methods.where: Azurite Mine from Act 4 (Niko, level 14+), or trade
methods.use_when: Themed combinations — Jagged/Serrated phys weapons, Dense ES gear, Bound minion helms — or blocking a polluting mod class before a reroll.
methods.avoid_when: A single guaranteed mod is enough; essence is cheaper and certain.

methods.name: Beastcrafting (Einhar)
methods.what: Fixed recipes at the Menagerie; the load-bearing one is the Craicic Chimeral imprint: snapshot a magic item, restore it after a failed gamble
methods.where: Einhar encounters from Act 2; beasts bank account-wide
methods.use_when: Insurance before regal-gambling a valuable magic item (+1/+1 wand, T1-mod base); item splitting and aspects are niche extras.
methods.avoid_when: As a primary mod source — beastcrafting protects and duplicates more than it creates.

methods.name: Harvest (Sacred Grove)
methods.what: Lifeforce-priced targeted crafts: 'reforge [tag]' (a smarter chaos), 'reforge keeping prefixes/suffixes' (fix half an item), augments at the top end
methods.where: maps only — portals in map areas; not available during the campaign
methods.use_when: First maps onward: upgrading worn rares in place without losing what's already good.
methods.avoid_when: During the campaign (doesn't exist yet), or when an essence covers the need for less.

methods.name: Veiled mods (Jun / Betrayal)
methods.what: Unveiling veiled drops permanently widens the bench (hybrid crafts: MS+onslaught, phys+hybrid); veiled items carry strong mods directly
methods.use_when: Early maps: unveil everything — the bench options compound; wear the good unveils meanwhile.
methods.avoid_when: Chasing one specific unveil — it's a slow lottery; craft around it instead.

methods.name: Rog (Expedition)
methods.what: Rog's gamble UI upgrades items through add/reroll steps for free — routinely outputs gear you couldn't craft this cheap
methods.where: Expedition encounters in campaign zones and maps
methods.use_when: Any weak slot while leveling; boots/gloves/helm especially. Take the item offer, follow the upgrade chain, stop while ahead.
methods.avoid_when: Weapons for specific bases — Rog offers what he offers.

methods.name: Eldritch implicits
methods.what: Grasping/Exceptional embers and ichors set deterministic implicit tiers on non-influenced armour pieces
methods.where: endgame maps with Searing Exarch / Eater of Worlds influence
methods.use_when: Gear has settled (75+): implicits stack power onto items you're keeping anyway.
methods.avoid_when: Anything still turning over every few levels.

methods.name: Recombinators
methods.what: Merge two items' mod sets into one base — the advanced consolidation tool
methods.where: availability varies by league — VERIFY: whether 3.29 has them, at launch
methods.use_when: Two half-good items of the same class late in consolidation.
methods.avoid_when: Before the mechanics of the current league's version are understood — outcomes burn both inputs.
